L2.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where newly created user accounts through the public registration feature inherit all configured default permissions — including administrative privileges — without any server-side restriction. When public registration is enabled and the default user configuration includes elevated access, any unauthenticated visitor can create an account that is automatically granted full administrative control over the application, including file management, user management, and server configuration.

L3.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where newly created user accounts through the public registration feature inherit all configured default permissions — including administrative privileges — without any server-side restriction. When public registration is enabled and the default user configuration includes elevated access, any unauthenticated visitor can create an account that is automatically granted full administrative control over the application, including file management, user management, and server configuration.

ground_truth.reason: The signup handler blindly applies all default user settings, including the admin permission flag, to newly registered accounts without stripping elevated privileges, allowing any unauthenticated visitor to register as a full administrator.
